CREATE TABLE ldap_entries (dn VARCHAR(255) PRIMARY KEY, cn VARCHAR(255), mail VARCHAR(255), telephoneNumber VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO ldap_entries VALUES ('cn=John Doe o=opl c=BG', 'John Doe', 'john.doe@example.com', '123-45-00');
INSERT INTO ldap_entries VALUES ('cn=Jane Smith o=opl c=BG', 'Jane Smith', 'jane.smith@example.com', '032-633710');
INSERT INTO ldap_entries VALUES ('cn=Bob Brown o=opl c=BG', 'Bob Brown', 'bob.brown@example.com', '048 850 760');

CREATE PROCEDURE sam_ldap_modify (IN s1 VARCHAR, IN s2 VARCHAR) BEGIN DECLARE res, vec ANY; DECLARE _from_modify INTEGER; vec := VECTOR('dn', CONCAT('cn=', s1, ' ', s2, ',o=opl,c=BG'), 'mail', VECTOR('new_email@techno-link.com'), 'telephoneNumber', VECTOR('111-11-11', '222-22-22', '333-33-33')); _from_modify := ldap_modify('ldap://localhost:389', 0, vec, NULL); RETURN (_from_modify); END;

CREATE PROCEDURE update_ldap_email (IN old_email VARCHAR, IN new_email VARCHAR) BEGIN DECLARE res, vec ANY; DECLARE _from_modify INTEGER; SET vec = VECTOR('mail', VECTOR(new_email)); _from_modify := ldap_modify('ldap://localhost:389', (SELECT dn FROM ldap_entries WHERE mail = old_email), vec, NULL); RETURN (_from_modify); END;

CREATE PROCEDURE modify_ldap_telephone (IN name VARCHAR, IN new_numbers VARCHAR) BEGIN DECLARE res, vec ANY; DECLARE _from_modify INTEGER; SET vec = VECTOR('telephoneNumber', VECTOR(new_numbers)); _from_modify := ldap_modify('ldap://localhost:389', (SELECT dn FROM ldap_entries WHERE cn = name), vec, NULL); RETURN (_from_modify); END;
SELECT * FROM ldap_entries WHERE dn IN (SELECT dn FROM ldap_entries WHERE cn LIKE 'Jane%');
UPDATE ldap_entries SET mail = 'old_email@example.com' WHERE cn = 'Bob Brown';
